intent: We are not building the Forge Agent architecture. We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Consensus Dev Agent — a native macOS AI coding agent with a two-process architecture (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses a dual-LLM consensus engine (Claude + GPT-4o, Claude arbitrates) to autonomously decompose build intents into ordered PRD plans, generate implementation and tests for each PR via parallel generation with 3-pass review, execute CI, and gate on operator approval before merging. The system encompasses 12 TRD-specified subsystems: macOS Application Shell (TRD-1), Consensus Engine (TRD-2), Build Pipeline with 8 stages and 3-pass review (TRD-3), Multi-Agent Coordination via BuildLedger (TRD-4), GitHub Integration Layer (TRD-5), Holistic Code Review with 5 lenses (TRD-6), TRD Development Workflow with 8 phases (TRD-7), UI/UX Design System (TRD-8), Mac CI Runner Infrastructure (TRD-9), Document Store with FAISS vector retrieval (TRD-10), Security Threat Model with mandatory SEC-* controls (TRD-11), Backend Runtime Startup and Version Handshake (TRD-12), and Recovery/State Management with checkpoint-first architecture (TRD-13). All code must conform to the security controls in TRD-11 (authoritative over all other TRDs), the two-process isolation boundary (Swift never calls LLM APIs, Python never reads Keychain), and the engineering standards in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

Clarifications:
  - resolve any constraints or undefined issues during the PRD creation.
  - chose the best naming convention based on best practices
  - resolve any constraints or undefined issues during the PRD creation.
  - resolve any constraints or undefined issues during the PRD creation.
  - resolve any constraints or undefined issues during the PRD creation.
  - resolve any constraints or undefined issues during the PRD creation.

prd_plan.title: Cross-TRD Architecture Baseline and Canonical Contracts
Security Control Framework and Trust Boundary Enforcement
Swift macOS Application Shell Foundation
Authentication, Keychain, and Session Lifecycle
Authenticated XPC/Unix-Socket Bridge and Message Protocol
Backend Runtime Startup, Health Model, and Graceful Shutdown
Version Handshake and Compatibility Enforcement
Document Store, Parsing, FAISS Retrieval, and Context Assembly
Consensus Engine Core with Parallel Generation and Arbitration
Build Pipeline State Model, ThreadStateStore, and Stage Framework
GitHub Integration Layer and Repository Bootstrap
Operator Gate System and Approval Flow
ScopeStage and PRDPlanStage for Intent Decomposition
PRD Generation and PR Planning Stages
Code Generation Stage with Dependency Context and Path Safety
Three-Pass Review Stage and Security Review Tooling
Local Test Stage, Failure Routing, and CI Gate Stage
BuildLedger Multi-Agent Coordination
Recovery, Save/Restore, Continue, and Checkpoint-First Architecture
Holistic Code Review Workflow
TRD Development Workflow
Swift UI/UX Design System and Operator Experience
Document Import, Preview, and Project Document Management UI
Mac CI Runner Infrastructure and Workflow Generation
Observability, Audit Trail, and Operational Diagnostics
End-to-End Integration, Compliance Validation, and Release Readiness

1.title: Establish repository directory structure and path namespace conventions
1.summary: Create the canonical directory layout that all subsequent PRs will use. Defines forge-standards/ for architecture contracts and ADRs, forge-runtime/ for operational state (ledger, journals), forge-docs/ reserved for operator TRD documents, src/consensus_engine/ for Python backend, ConsensusShell/ for Swift shell, and tests/ mirroring src/. Includes a PATH_REGISTRY.json machine-readable path map, a REPOSITORY_NAMESPACE.md reference document, and .gitkeep scaffolds for all directories. Resolves the TRD-4/TRD-5 path collision (forge-docs/ vs forge-runtime/) identified in the PRD.

2.title: Define cross-TRD precedence rules, ADR template, and two-process isolation boundary
2.summary: Create the ADR template and foundational ADRs that codify governance. ADR-001 establishes the precedence hierarchy: TRD-11 security controls > owning TRD on domain-specific matters > shared contracts > implementation decisions. ADR-002 codifies the two-process isolation boundary rule (Swift never calls LLM APIs, Python never reads Keychain). Includes a PRECEDENCE_RULES.md policy document and a Python precedence resolution helper module that downstream code can query to validate decisions. Each ADR follows a Status/Context/Decision/Consequences structure stored in forge-standards/adrs/.

3.title: Add canonical naming conventions and glossary for shared domain terms
3.summary: Defines canonical names for entities, statuses, identifiers, files, and process-boundary terms to eliminate cross-TRD naming collisions. Establishes ownership of shared vocabulary (e.g., 'PRSpec' vs 'PRPlanEntry', 'BuildThread' vs 'BuildLedger') and produces a GLOSSARY.md and CANONICAL_NAMING.md that all subsequent schema PRs must reference. Includes a Python module with canonical name constants.

4.title: Define semantic versioning policy and VERSION file contract
4.summary: Establish the single-source-of-truth VERSION file at the repository root with semver (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) and define the version handshake contract between Swift shell and Python backend (from TRD-12). Includes a Python utility to read/validate VERSION, a version compatibility matrix (shell version range vs backend version range), and ADR-003 documenting version alignment across Swift Info.plist, Python pyproject.toml, and the VERSION file.

5.title: Implement shared contract base classes, registry, and schema export tooling
5.summary: Creates the foundational contract infrastructure: base dataclass with serialization/deserialization helpers, a schema registry that can programmatically register, load, and validate any contract by name, and schema export tooling that validates dataclass-to-JSON-schema consistency and produces reproducible exports. All downstream schema PRs will use these base classes and register with this registry.

6.title: Define global identifier and version primitives
6.summary: Adds shared contract primitives for PRD IDs, PR numbers, thread IDs, review IDs, stage IDs, schema versions, semver parsing, and compatibility ranges that all downstream schemas import. These are the atomic types referenced across TRDs. Produces both Python dataclasses (using base from PR-5) and JSON schema definitions.

7.title: Define canonical error taxonomy and error code registry
7.summary: Establish the unified error taxonomy spanning all 13 TRDs. Defines error domains (SECURITY, PIPELINE, CONSENSUS, LEDGER, GITHUB, REVIEW, DOCUMENT, RUNTIME, RECOVERY), severity levels (FATAL, ERROR, WARNING, INFO), and a numeric code registry with reserved ranges per TRD (e.g., SEC-* codes 1000-1099 from TRD-11, PIPE-* 2000-2099 from TRD-3). Produces Python enums/dataclasses registered with the schema registry and a JSON schema file. Includes ADR-004 documenting the error taxonomy design decision and why TRD-11 SEC-* codes take precedence.

8.title: Define pipeline stage status and ledger status enums
8.summary: Resolve the naming collision between TRD-3 PRSpec.status and TRD-4 pr_entries.status by defining two distinct enums: PipelineStageStatus for TRD-3 build pipeline stages and LedgerPRStatus for TRD-4 coordination. Produces ADR-005 documenting the status enum split decision. All enums registered with the contract registry and exported as JSON schema.

9.title: Define XPC envelope schema and authenticated message contract
9.summary: Define the canonical XPC envelope structure used for all Swift shell ↔ Python backend communication over the authenticated Unix socket. The envelope includes message_id (UUIDv4), message_type (enum), timestamp (ISO-8601), payload (type-specific JSON), hmac_signature (for TRD-11 SEC-* authenticated channel), and protocol_version. Produces a JSON Schema file, Python dataclass with serialization/deserialization and HMAC validation, and ADR-006 documenting envelope design. This is the foundational IPC contract referenced by TRD-1, TRD-12, and TRD-11.

10.title: Define backend startup and version handshake contracts
10.summary: Introduces the canonical startup sequence, capability advertisement, protocol negotiation, minimum compatible versions, and rejection semantics for shell-backend startup and version alignment. Builds on XPC envelope (PR-9) and version primitives (PR-6). Produces handshake schemas and a STARTUP_HANDSHAKE.md contract document.

11.title: Define build pipeline stage input/output contracts
11.summary: Define canonical dataclasses for all 8 pipeline stage outputs (intent_parsed, prd_generated, prd_approved, pr_plan_created, code_generated, review_passed, ci_passed, merge_complete) with their input/output schemas, transition invariants, and approval gate requirements. Uses PipelineStageStatus from PR-8. Produces a PIPELINE_STAGE_CATALOG.md reference document.

12.title: Define BuildLedger record schema and ownership contract
12.summary: Resolve the schema ownership ambiguity between TRD-3 (BuildThread.pr_plans_by_prd) and TRD-4 (BuildLedger.pr_plans_by_prd) by establishing the BuildLedger as the single authoritative store and BuildThread as a read-through projection. Define canonical dataclasses for BuildLedger records: PRDEntry, PRPlanEntry (using LedgerPRStatus from PR-8), DependencyEdge, and the top-level BuildLedger structure. Includes the ledger file path contract (forge-runtime/BUILD_LEDGER.json), journal entry schema (forge-runtime/journals/), and ADR-007 documenting the single-owner decision.

13.title: Define holistic review issue schema with 5 lenses and 3-pass structure
13.summary: Define the canonical schema for review issues produced by the holistic code review system (TRD-6). Each ReviewIssue has an id, lens (correctness, security, architecture, style, test_coverage), severity (blocker, warning, suggestion), file_path, line_range, description, suggested_fix, and remediation_state. Define the ReviewReport aggregate containing issues grouped by lens, pass_number (1-3), and an overall verdict (pass/fail/conditional). Produces JSON schemas and a REVIEW_CONTRACTS.md document.

14.title: Define consensus engine request/response schemas
14.summary: Define the canonical schemas for the dual-LLM consensus engine (TRD-2). Includes ConsensusRequest (prompt, context, required_agreement_level), ConsensusResponse (claude_response, gpt4o_response, arbitration_result, confidence_score, reasoning), and ArbitrationDecision (chosen_response, divergence_summary, arbitrator=claude). Produces ADR-008 explaining why Claude is the canonical arbitrator.

15.title: Define document store and FAISS retrieval schemas
15.summary: Define the canonical schemas for the document store with FAISS vector retrieval (TRD-10). Includes DocumentRecord (doc_id, doc_type enum covering TRD/PRD/ADR/code/test, content_hash, embedding_id, metadata), RetrievalQuery (query_text, top_k, filter_by_type), and RetrievalResult (ranked list of DocumentRecord with similarity scores). Produces a DOCSTORE_CONTRACTS.md document. These schemas support the RAG context pipeline feeding into the consensus engine.

16.title: Define GitHub integration contract schemas
16.summary: Define the canonical schemas for the GitHub integration layer (TRD-5). Includes PRCreateRequest (repo, branch, title, body, files, base_branch), PRCreateResponse (pr_number, url, status), CIStatusPayload (pr_number, check_runs, overall_status), and MergeRequest (pr_number, merge_method, required_approvals). These schemas define the contract between the pipeline stages and the GitHub adapter without implementing the adapter itself.

17.title: Define operator approval, merge gate, and audit trail contracts
17.summary: Adds the canonical approval decision, merge authorization, operator acknowledgement, and audit-trail schemas that govern human-in-the-loop gating before merge. These sit at the intersection of review (PR-13), ledger (PR-12), and GitHub integration (PR-16), codifying when and how operator approval is required and recorded.

18.title: Define checkpoint, recovery, and state transition contracts
18.summary: Define the canonical schemas for the checkpoint-first recovery system (TRD-13). Includes Checkpoint (checkpoint_id, pipeline_stage, thread_id, timestamp, state_snapshot, integrity_hash), RecoveryManifest (last_good_checkpoint, failed_stage, recovery_strategy enum), and StateTransition (from_stage, to_stage, preconditions_met, postconditions). Produces a RECOVERY_STATE_MODEL.md contract document. These schemas enable the pipeline to persist and resume from any stage boundary.

19.title: Define TRD-11 security controls registry and SEC-* validation contracts
19.summary: Codify all mandatory SEC-* controls from TRD-11 as machine-readable contracts. Includes SecurityControl dataclass (control_id like SEC-001, title, description, enforcement_point, automated_check_possible), the full registry of controls, a validation interface that downstream modules must call to assert compliance, and process-boundary prohibition rules (Swift keychain-only, Python LLM-only, secret handling). Produces ADR-009 listing each SEC-* control with its enforcement strategy, a SECURITY_CONTROL_MATRIX.md, and PROCESS_BOUNDARY_RULES.md.

20.title: Codify AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md engineering guardrails as machine-checkable acceptance rules
20.summary: Extract all engineering standards from AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md and codify them as machine-checkable acceptance rules. Includes rules like: max file length, required docstrings, no direct LLM calls from Swift, no Keychain access from Python, mandatory type hints, test coverage thresholds, commit message format, and PR size limits. Produces a GuardrailRule dataclass, a registry of all rules with check functions, an ACCEPTANCE_RULES.json policy file, and ADR-010 documenting which guardrails are automated vs manual-review-only.

21.title: Add pyproject.toml with dynamic versioning, dependencies, and tool configuration
21.summary: Create the Python project configuration that wires together all contract modules. pyproject.toml reads version dynamically from the VERSION file (PR-4), declares dependencies (pydantic for schema validation, dataclasses-json for serialization), dev dependencies (pytest, mypy, ruff), and configures tool settings (mypy strict mode, ruff rules matching CLAUDE.md style). Includes a py.typed marker and a minimal setup validation that imports all contract modules.

22.title: Create canonical interface catalog and cross-reference index
22.summary: Produce the single canonical interface catalog document that cross-references every schema, enum, contract, and ADR from PRs 2-20. Includes forge-standards/contracts/INTERFACE_CATALOG.md with tables mapping: schema name → owning TRD → JSON schema path → Python module path → ADR reference. Also includes a Python module that programmatically aggregates all registered schemas from the registry (PR-5) and validates completeness, plus a build script that regenerates the catalog and a machine-readable INTERFACE_CATALOG.json.

23.title: Integration tests: cross-schema validation, contract consistency, and precedence resolution
23.summary: Comprehensive integration test suite validating that all schemas, enums, error codes, and contracts are internally consistent. Tests include: no overlapping error code ranges, all JSON schemas validate against their Python dataclass round-trips, XPC envelope can wrap every stage output type, ledger records reference valid pipeline statuses, review issues reference valid lenses, checkpoint schemas can serialize full pipeline state, security controls cover all enforcement points, the interface catalog lists every registered schema, precedence resolution produces correct outcomes, naming conventions are consistently applied, and version alignment holds across VERSION/pyproject.toml. This is the acceptance gate for the entire PRD-001.
